explanatory_strength.rationale: Round-two review confirms the same croupier fee-on-AUM mechanism: FY2026 revenue ¥22.5B, AUM ¥1.34tn, success fees ~65% of combined fees, mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh versus FY2026 peak ¥317.33. The 2026-07-24 contract backfill clarifies cycle normalization and overlap controls but does not convert the story into quality-reinvestment economics: archetype remains croupier, moat partial, holdco profit flows through subsidiaries, and etf_product_pipeline still terminates at judgment values without a reinvestment rate or incremental ROIC bridge required by the quality_reinvestment route.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core operating facts reconcile from finfo2026 and Pnotice2026-1; evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-24 closes the additive ownership map with four mechanically valid calculation proofs and empty double_counting_flags. Targeted research response is absent (no research_response.json answers), so round-one persona gaps persist: live_price_confirmed false (Yahoo ¥757 equals BVPS ¥757.01 versus last exchange print ¥464 on 100 shares); yuho not mirrored locally; management_facts claims unverified; etf_product_pipeline lacks reinvestment rate, incremental ROIC, or distributable-cash runway despite owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf method_id.

downside_control.rationale: Asset-light balance sheet limits insolvency risk and contract low case still implies ¥1,176/sh versus ¥757 reference price (6.49% annualized). Permanent loss is not bounded at spot: earnings remain ~65% performance-fee exposed, there is no book floor for owner cash, TSE tape is non-continuous (two nonzero-volume days in twelve months), and performance_fee_cycle_reserve (base −¥140/sh) only partially captures peak-cycle risk. Pre-mortem and empty research loop leave execution risk unmitigated.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return 16.94% at ¥757 clears the 12% Buffett/Weschler hurdle on paper (low 6.49%, high 26.83%), and component base ¥2,264/sh implies large upside if price were real. Those paths depend on an unconfirmed quote that may be BVPS mislabeled, mid-cycle normalization holding, and judgment-heavy reinvestment and book-claim rows. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% and dive headline 53.33% at ¥464 are not executable on public feeds. Return is attractive only if price and liquidity are verified; conservative owner-earnings reinvestment proof remains incomplete.

claims.claim: Simplex Financial Holdings remains outside the classic Buffett/Weschler power zone: croupier archetype, partial moat, partial dhando, and cyclical success-fee earnings. Contract backfill improves the ownership map but does not make this a durable compounder with proven reinvestable owner earnings.

Mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh is the correct croupier anchor versus FY2026 peak ¥317.33, and core_fee_engine reconciles to ¥2,239/sh base with valid overlap controls, but etf_product_pipeline still lacks incremental ROIC or reinvestment-rate traces required by the quality_reinvestment route.

At unconfirmed ¥757 (likely BVPS not last trade), contract base return 16.94% per year may clear the 12% hurdle on paper, but illiquid TSE execution and peak success-fee cyclicality block approve. Watch pending live quote, liquidity, and filing-verified reinvestment economics; targeted research loop did not resolve round-one gaps.

strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural ETF adoption and retail equity re-rating make Simplex's Japan-first active and thematic pipeline a widening-moat fee compounder: FY2026 success fees reflect sustainable alpha and product pull-through rather than a cyclical peak, illiquidity permanently misprices the float, and patient owners will capture component value (~¥2,264/sh) as base fees compound with AUM regardless of short-term performance-fee normalization.
most_important_missing_fact: Live TSE quote with depth plus filing-verified reinvestment rate and incremental return on capital on retained owner cash, with parent-to-subsidiary dividend flow reconciled to separate durable base-fee owner earnings from cyclical success-fee earnings.
falsifiers: Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥100 for two consecutive fiscal years without a disclosed structural fee cut.
Success fees decline more than 30% year over year for two consecutive quarters with no offsetting base-fee or AUM growth.
Incremental return on capital on disclosed reinvestment stays below 12% while payout exceeds 80% of distributable owner cash.
TSE shows zero reported volume for two consecutive quarters with no disclosed block trade or liquidity program.
specialist_findings: Round two: no targeted research_response.json; round-one gaps on live price, reinvestment economics, and yuho verification remain open. Outside power zone (croupier archetype, moat partial, dhando partial; Buffett rules require compounder/holding_co/serial_acquirer/infrastructure plus stable/widening moat — only financial_services sleeve matches). Audited all four valuation-proof rows post backfill: core_fee_engine owner_cash_or_dividend_discount mechanics reconcile from ¥137.14 normalized owner cash with valid 7-year DCF to ¥2,239 base; etf_product_pipeline uses method_id owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf but trace stops at judgment pipeline_value_per_share (¥40–220) with no reinvestment rate, incremental ROIC, or distributable-cash runway — fails route required_evidence; net_financial_claims applies 0–17.2% claim_share haircut on BVPS for holdco pass-through (wide judgment); performance_fee_cycle_reserve quantifies peak-cycle reserve (~1.02× mid-cycle owner cash at base, success fees ~65% of combined fees). No comparables attached. Overlap controls pass (four unique keys, no double_counting_flags). Option scan: thematic ETF pipeline embedded_in_segment only. Capital requirement risk is earnings cyclicality and liquidity, not balance-sheet insolvency. Contract base annualized return 16.94% at ¥757 vs 12% hurdle; live_price_confirmed false, Yahoo ¥757 equals BVPS ¥757.01, last print ¥464 (100 shares) makes executable return unknowable. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% at ¥757 diverges from executive-summary ¥464 framing (53.33%) flagged by Milly.
